= CML fixtures

Example CML documents used by the spec suite. Each fixture exercises
one CML feature so regressions are easy to localize.

== Provenance

All fixtures in this directory are **synthetic** — written for this
gem, not scraped from upstream. They are licensed under the gem's
BSD-2-Clause license, not the upstream CC-BY-3.0.

(Scraping the full upstream corpus requires `bundle exec rake
fixtures:scrape`, which writes additional files into these
directories. Those would be CC-BY-3.0 and would carry a per-file
attribution header.)

== Layout

`schema3/molecular/`::  Molecules — atomArrays, bondArrays, formulas,
                        names, identifiers, properties, stereo, parity
`schema3/compchem/`::   CompChem modules — jobList / job / init /
                        calc / final / env, parameters, properties
`schema3/dictionary/`:: Dictionaries — entries with definitions
`schema3/unit/`::       Unit lists — SI and non-SI units
`schema24/`::           Schema 2.4 examples (including crystal)

== Running

Every fixture is round-tripped by
`spec/chemicalml/fixtures_round_trip_spec.rb`:

  bundle exec rspec spec/chemicalml/fixtures_round_trip_spec.rb

== Adding a fixture

1. Pick a feature not yet covered (e.g. `<spectrum>`, `<peakList>`).
2. Write a minimal `.cml` file demonstrating the feature.
3. Run the round-trip spec to confirm it loads and re-serializes.
